claim: Raw remote stderr bytes (including ANSI escapes / control characters) are embedded verbatim into diagnostic strings; a hostile or misconfigured server can inject terminal escape sequences into text that F21 will eventually render (terminal-escape injection into UI/log surfaces).
fix: Strip control characters (C0/C1, ESC sequences) from stderrTail before embedding it in any diagnostic; keep the 400-char bound. A small inline sanitizer in remote-client (it must not import src/main's stripAnsi).
resolution: sanitizeStderr (bootstrap.ts:37, C0/C1 incl. ESC stripped, escaped-regex verified byte-clean) now applied at BOTH stderr accumulation sites (connect :177, provision :299) and to every onDiagnostic line (:179), so no control byte can reach a diagnostic; verified by source read + typecheck + full suite green on the re-derived implement gate.

claim: The safe-remote-path charset admits '..' segments, so a tampered registry entry could aim remoteAgentDir at '~/../../other' — no privilege boundary is crossed (the upload runs as the configured user over their own ssh auth), but rejecting traversal is cheap defense-in-depth.
fix: Reject any '/../' or leading '../' segment (and a bare '..') in checkRemotePath.
resolution: checkRemotePath now rejects '..'/'../'/'/../'/trailing '/..' segments (ssh-command.ts:74) with a field-naming CONV-001 error; verified by source read + suite green (existing charset tests unaffected).

claim: Two concurrent saveNamedAgents calls against the SAME filePath share the fixed temp name '<filePath>.tmp': B's writeFile can truncate/interleave with A's, and A's rename can promote B's half-written bytes — a torn registry file the atomic-save design exists to prevent. The API is an unserialized module function, so nothing enforces the single-writer assumption.
fix: Make the temp name unique per call (pid + counter or crypto suffix) so concurrent saves cannot collide; last rename still wins whole-file.
resolution: saveNamedAgents temp name is now unique per call: pid base36 + monotonic counter (agents-store.ts:31,:38) — concurrent saves cannot share a temp file; rename still promotes whole-file. TEST-2019's no-survivor assertion stays green.

claim: Exit 127 conflates 'artifact absent' with 'remote node binary missing' (the shell's command-not-found from 'exec node' is also 127): with no node on the remote PATH the flow classifies absent, uploads, relaunches, gets 127 again, and reports provision-ineffective with a diagnostic that blames the artifact ('did not take effect') — misdirecting the user from the actual fix (install node / fix the login-shell PATH).
fix: Code-side now: the provision-ineffective (and second absent) diagnostic must name the node-missing possibility explicitly. Deeper (deferred follow-up, needs a tests-phase amendment of the frozen launch-command pin): give the probe a distinct sentinel for node-missing, e.g. 'command -v node >/dev/null || exit 96'.
resolution: The claimed defect (misdirecting diagnostic) is fixed: the provision-ineffective diagnostic now explicitly names the node-missing possibility and the 127 conflation (bootstrap.ts:351). The deeper probe-sentinel split (command -v node || exit 96) requires amending the frozen TEST-2015 launch-command pin — recorded as a deferred follow-up in docs/superpowers/0020-ssh-tunnel-provisioned-bootstrap-review-followups.md (written at doc-sync), never to be slipped in silently.

claim: No default timeout is deliberate (2FA prompting must not be raced), but the spec never STATES the resulting contract: a signal-less connectAgent/connectWithProvisioning against a host that accepts the connection and then wedges (agent launches, never emits a hello) hangs FOREVER. The cancellation obligation on the caller is implicit — a consumer (F21) can ship a hang.
fix: State the contract explicitly in the spec/feature doc: 'no built-in timeout; the caller MUST own cancellation via options.signal'. Optionally add a convenience options.timeoutMs that wraps an internal AbortController (additive, non-breaking). Deferred follow-up; no frozen-test change required for the doc route.

claim: A remote login shell that prints to stdout on non-interactive exec (a .bashrc echo — common on Debian-pattern sshd builds that source rc files) corrupts the length-prefixed frame stream BEFORE the agent hello; the flow correctly classifies fatal (bad-json/frame-too-large), but the diagnostic never hints that shell-rc stdout noise is the classic cause, leaving the user to decode framing internals.
fix: Code-side now: when a pre-hello decode failure classifies fatal, append a hint naming shell-rc stdout noise as a likely cause. Deeper robustness (skip-to-first-frame markers) is explicitly NOT wanted (masks corruption) — record as a known v1 limitation in the feature doc.
location: src/remote-client/bootstrap.ts connectAgent (pre-hello decode failures); spec REQ-011
resolution: The claimed defect (no rc-noise hint) is fixed: pre-hello decode failures (bad-json/bad-message/frame-too-large/decoder-dead) now append RC_NOISE_HINT naming shell-rc stdout noise and its remedy (bootstrap.ts:43,:162). Deeper skip-to-first-frame robustness deliberately rejected (masks corruption); recorded as a known v1 limitation in the feature doc at doc-sync.

claim: Version-embedded artifact names mean every client upgrade adds a new termhalla-agent-<v>.cjs under ~/.termhalla/agent with no garbage collection — unbounded (if tiny: ~52KB/version) accumulation on every remote host.
fix: Deferred follow-up (F21/integration): after a successful handshake, prune termhalla-agent-*.cjs files other than the active version (needs its own tests-phase work; do not slip it in silently).

claim: The shim derives the remote-command slice via argv.indexOf(destination); an identityFile value string-equal to the destination (e.g. -i ci@host ci@host cmd) would match the earlier -i VALUE and misparse a valid builder argv into a shim exit 12. Unreachable by any current frozen test input (tests use filesystem identity paths), so purely a latent test-substrate weakness.
fix: Track the parser cursor and slice from it (rest = argv.slice(i)) instead of indexOf. The shim is inside the frozen test root — apply only through a sanctioned tests-phase amendment (a future feature touching this substrate), never silently during implement.
